multi.q: While driving on a narrow road at night, a vehicle ahead of you has been signalling to turn right for some time and is moving slowly. What should you do?
options: Pull over to the right and honk your horn
Wait for the signal to cancel before overtaking

multi.q: When are you allowed to park your vehicle on a road without turning off the engine?
options: If you park your vehicle for below 5 minutes
Keep going, you have the right of way
Always maintain the maximum legal speed
Drive slowly only during school hours

multi.q: Pedestrians should not cross the road from behind a vehicle or near a bend. Why?
options: They should cross where there is a road sign
It can be difficult for pedestrians
It can be scary for children
Drivers of other vehicles cannot see the person crossing the road

multi.q: What should you do when you approach a staggered junction?
options: Use hazard warning lights to proceed
Use flashing headlights to pass the junction
Maintain your speed and honk your horn

multi.q: When going straight ahead on a roundabout…
options: Use flashing headlights to indicate you are going straight

multi.q: Your vehicle pulls to one side when braking. What should you do?
options: Use the handbrake at the same time to stop the vehicle

multi.q: To prevent brake fade when going downhill?
options: Select neutral for a short distance
Select the appropriate high-power gear and apply the brake only when necessary
Descend in top gear and apply the brake only when necessary

multi.q: What should you do if the front tire bursts while you are driving on the road?
options: Loosen the grip on the steering wheel
Lock the steering wheel to any extreme position and apply the brake
Hold on to the steering wheel and stop safely using the brake

multi.q: What should you do if your vehicle skids while driving?
options: Turn the steering wheel left and right
Turn the steering wheel in the direction the car is skidding
Turn the steering wheel in the opposite direction of the skid

multi.q: What causes hard steering?
Driving at high speeds…
options: Takes less time to complete your journey
Reduces the time you have to respond and avoid an accident
Slow down using only the gears
Take pre-planned action in using the brakes
As shown in the registration certificate
None of the above is correct
Foot rest for pillion rider on both sides
Using brake and clutch in high torque gear
If there is no vehicle coming from the other side, you can overtake
If there is no vehicle coming from the other side, you can overtake
Indicates a crossing or cross road

multi.q: What do ladder hatching signs in the middle of the road indicate?
options: Equal to a median and overtaking is prohibited
Overtaking is allowed if there is no vehicle coming from the opposite direction
Overtaking is allowed on both sides
Vehicles can be parked in the hatched section

multi.q: What do the reverse hatch signs on the border line indicate?
options: Not intended for parking or driving; meant as a pedestrian area
Apply sudden brakes to slow down
Continue at the same speed if you feel safe
Press the brake as well as the clutch to avoid skidding
Give way to traffic coming from other roads into the intersection
Proceed by giving the correct signal and honking
Give priority to vehicles approaching the intersection from your right, then proceed after giving the required signals
Give priority to vehicles approaching the intersection from your left, then proceed after giving the required signals
Maintaining a safe distance from the vehicle in front
Maintaining a distance of at least 7 meters from the vehicle in front
Driving dangerously close behind the vehicle in front
Driving dangerously ahead of another vehicle
Driving with caution, anticipating violations of traffic rules and road signs by drivers and other road users
Driving with the sole aim of reaching the destination without regard to road signs
Driving with the assumption that other road users will be careful about your safety

multi.q: When using a vehicle with left-hand steering control…
options: Use a mechanical or electrical signalling device
Left-hand steering vehicles are not allowed to drive

multi.q: Use of red road markings as a warning is…
options: To help people understand the danger
To understand the smooth flow of traffic
To know the roads with poor visibility

multi.q: Double stop lines are applied…
options: At traffic signals and in front of pedestrian crossings
At junctions controlled by stop signs
Driving in a zigzag manner and passing too close to vehicles

multi.q: What is the main purpose of first aid?
options: To reduce pain and prioritise accident treatment
To prevent the injury from getting worse and its consequences
To protect the injured person from further harm

multi.q: When you drive a vehicle from a parallel parking space onto the street, what should you do?
options: Proceed with caution after making sure that there are no vehicles in the vicinity that could cause a hazard
Signal to other traffic and proceed with caution
Blind spots and traffic in all directions
Honk your horn to alert other drivers
Drive through the intersection at a reduced speed
Wait for traffic to clear before entering
Interact with them to establish your position
Ignore them and maintain your speed and distance
Try to pass them to demonstrate your competence
Follow closely to make sure they don't cut you off
Maintain a safe distance and be alert
If the vehicle is being driven at high speed
If the driver is distracted or tired
If the vehicle is being driven under the influence of drugs or alcohol

multi.q: A vehicle whose gross vehicle weight is above what limit is called a heavy vehicle (heavy goods vehicle or heavy passenger motor vehicle)?
"Road user" means who among the following? (I) A person driving a vehicle (II) A person travelling on a road in a vehicle (III) A person travelling on a road other than in a vehicle (IV) A pedestrian

619.q: Which of the following is meant by "traffic"? (I) Includes all types of vehicles and other traffic (II) Includes pedestrians and processions (III) Includes animals ridden or in groups (IV) Includes all types of traffic using any road for the purpose of travel

620.q: Which of the following is meant by "traffic island"? (I) A physical system constructed at an intersection to control vehicular traffic (II) Physical systems at or near an intersection to control vehicular traffic (III) Also includes road markings marked on a vehicular lane (IV) Physical systems and road markings marked on the vehicular lane at or near an intersection to control vehicular traffic

621.q: Which of the following is meant by "right of way"? (I) The right of a vehicle to proceed lawfully in preference to another vehicle (II) The right of any road user to proceed lawfully in preference to another vehicle or any other road user (III) The right of a vehicle or any other road user to proceed lawfully in preference to another vehicle (IV) The right of a vehicle or any other road user to proceed lawfully in preference to another approaching vehicle or road user

622.q: "Stopping" means stopping a vehicle for a very short period of time or… (I) Stopping to allow passengers to board or alight (II) Stopping for the purpose of loading or unloading goods (III) Stopping either for the purpose of loading or unloading passengers or goods (IV) None of the above conditions are prescribed

623.q: "Traffic marking" means road markings provided on the … of a road

624.q: Which of the following is meant by "road"? (I) Bridges, tunnels, corridors, ferry facilities, interchanges, roundabouts, traffic lights (II) Road dividers, all traffic lanes, acceleration and deceleration lanes, medians, strips, overpasses (III) Underpasses, approaches, access roads, off ramps, toll plazas, a road under construction (IV) Any private road

multi.q: Which of the following is the duty of a driver? (I) Keep a good lookout and keep his attention on the road and traffic and avoid any activity that may distract him (II) Take precautions for the safety of the most vulnerable road users such as pedestrians, cyclists, children, the elderly and the disabled (III) Ensure that his vehicle does not cause any obstruction or unnecessary inconvenience to other road users or occupants of any property when moving or stationary (IV) Ensure that his vision and hearing are not obstructed by passengers, animals, other equipment in the vehicle or the condition of the vehicle
Select the most appropriate statement regarding the duties of a driver
options: Viewing digital motion pictures, videos and route navigation while driving is not allowed
Except where route navigation is required, you cannot watch digital motion pictures and videos while driving
It's okay to watch digital motion pictures or videos while driving on the highway
It's okay to watch digital motion pictures or videos while driving an electric vehicle
Take the vehicle to the service centre immediately
Move the vehicle off the road as quickly as possible
Do not stop the vehicle on the road even for a moment
Reduce the speed of the vehicle and proceed with caution

627.q: If a road is divided into several lanes for smooth traffic, which of the following statements is correct? (I) A driver is allowed to change lanes on the road only after giving the signal using the correct hand signal or indicator (II) If any lane on the road is specially marked for a category of vehicles, only that category of vehicle is allowed to drive on that lane (III) If a lane is marked for a specific purpose, no other vehicle is allowed to drive on that lane (IV) A continuous yellow line cannot be crossed, but a continuous white line can be crossed

options: At the junctions, the vehicle coming from the left side has priority, i.e. B (red car) has priority
At the junctions, the vehicle coming from the right side has priority, i.e. A (green car) has priority
Since the roads are of equal importance, both have equal rights

multi.q: At the junction of equal importance roads without a signal or police control shown in the figure, which car has priority?
options: Vehicle B (red) has priority because it is about to turn
Since the roads are of equal importance, both have equal right of way, so proceed with caution
Vehicle A (green) has entered the intersection and is going straight ahead, so vehicle A has priority over vehicle B
Both vehicles should stop and proceed with caution

multi.q: Which car has priority at a roundabout?
options: Car A has priority because it is going straight ahead
Car B has priority because it has already entered the roundabout
None of the above is correct

multi.q: At a junction with a traffic control signal: (I) The vehicle must slow down and obey the instructions given by the traffic control signals (II) At an intersection with a red light, the vehicle must stop before the stop line before the pedestrian crossing (III) If the stop line is not marked or is not visible, the vehicle must stop before the pedestrian crossing (IV) If the pedestrian crossing is not marked, the vehicle must stop before the second (secondary) traffic signal

Which of the following statements is correct in the case of overtaking? (I) Overtaking is only allowed on the right side of a vehicle (II) In multi-lane traffic, it is legal to overtake with caution in the left lane if the vehicles are moving in the same direction (III) In the case where the vehicle to be overtaken is moving in the middle of the road and is signalling to either turn right or make a "U" turn, it is safe to overtake that vehicle on the left side (IV) The vehicle to be overtaken is stationary and it is safe to pass from the left side

Mr. A, who was supposed to turn onto the left side road, noticed that he had passed the road he was supposed to turn on when his car had gone about 50 meters. In this situation, which of the following should Mr. A do?
options: Reverse the vehicle until it reaches the side road it has passed
No motor vehicle is allowed to reverse such a distance on a public road, so it is not allowed to reverse
In rural areas, he is not allowed to reverse the vehicle because reversing is restricted only in the city or municipality
He can reverse the vehicle during the day on multi-lane roads and highways

multi.q: Which statement regarding parking is incorrect?
options: No vehicle shall be parked in a place where parking is prohibited
No vehicle shall be parked on a road or part of a road where the maximum speed limit is 50 km/h
No vehicle shall be parked 100 meters before or after an intersection or junction
No vehicle shall be parked on a footpath, cycle track or pedestrian crossing
No vehicle shall be parked in the opposite direction of another parked vehicle
No vehicle shall be parked on a continuous yellow line painted on the curb of the road
No vehicle may be parked in any place where "No Parking" is not posted
No vehicle shall be parked on a footpath

640.q: When used for emergency duty, emergency vehicles may be equipped with: (i) Multi-tone horn (siren) (ii) Multi-coloured light with flasher (iii) An emergency call or an alarm (iv) The vehicle may be used for any other purpose

641.q: Which of the following can an emergency vehicle do? (i) Run a red traffic signal (ii) Exceed the speed limit (iii) Disobey "No Entry" or "One Way" (iv) Drive on the shoulder of the road

options: Fire engines, ambulances, police vehicles, state government vehicles
Ambulances, fire engines, police vehicles, VIP vehicles
VIP vehicles, ambulances, fire engines, police vehicles
Fire engines, ambulances, police vehicles, VIP vehicles

multi.q: What should a driver do if he sees an emergency vehicle overtaking his vehicle? (i) Move to the left side of the road as soon as possible (ii) Stop if necessary and remain stationary until the emergency vehicle passes (iii) Maintain a distance of at least 50 meters from the emergency vehicle (iv) Run right behind the emergency vehicle

Which of the following should be done if the vehicle breaks down? (i) Turn on the hazard warning lamp (ii) Place a reflective warning triangle 50 meters ahead or before the bend (iii) Place some stones on the road to attract the attention of other road users (iv) Place some leaves and branches behind the vehicle

644.q: What should be done if a vehicle is involved in a minor accident with no injuries to the passengers? (i) Take photographs of the accident (ii) Move the vehicle to the side of the road and place a warning triangle (iii) Call the police station if necessary (iv) Drivers should not leave the scene of the accident until all matters related to the accident have been resolved

multi.q: What should a driver do if a vehicle is involved in a major accident with injuries to the passengers? (i) Drive away from the scene and report it to the police station (ii) Call for medical assistance and the police (iii) Pass on the driver's name, address, phone number, insurance company, policy number, driving license number, and vehicle registration number to the relevant authorities (iv) Wait for the police to arrive and take the injured to the hospital

What are the points to be observed while towing a vehicle? (i) Two, three and four wheelers can be towed (ii) Two wheelers should not be towed (iii) The distance between the towing vehicle and the vehicle being towed should be 5 meters (iv) The maximum speed of the towing vehicle should be 25 km/h while displaying the "On Tow" board

646.q: Which of the following statements is correct regarding the registration plates of the vehicle? (i) The registration plates at the front and rear of the vehicle shall be clearly visible and legible (ii) No letter, word, picture or symbol other than the registration number shall be written on the registration plates (iii) No goods or other articles shall be placed on a motor vehicle in such a way as to completely or partially obscure the registration plate (iv) No vehicle shall be driven or parked on a public road without displaying the registration plates

647.q: As per Section 19, which of the following is not a ground for disqualification or cancellation of licence? (i) Habitual offender (ii) Persistent drunkard or addict to NDPS (iii) Using vehicle for committing cognizable offence (iv) Unlawful modification of vehicle

648.q: Driving a vehicle with a load exceeding the permissible weight is an offence under the M.V. Act. Which of the following is true regarding Section 194? (i) Loading of excess weight in a goods vehicle is an offence punishable by a fine of Rs. 20,000 and an additional fine of Rs. 2,000 for each ton in excess (ii) The vehicle must be unloaded before proceeding after legal action

651.q: Which of the following is correct regarding a green signal? (i) Be careful when starting the vehicle (ii) Make sure that vehicles from other directions have left (iii) Since the signal is green, you can proceed without looking at anything else
